research_problem.value: The central challenge is how to acquire and utilize high-quality dense rewards at scale.
research_problem.evidence: We identify the central challenge as how to acquire and utilize high-quality dense rewards at scale...

current_limitations.value: Existing methods either need to build complicated human annotation pipelines or rely on estimation-based methods.
current_limitations.evidence: Existing methods either need to build complicated human annotation pipelines (Lightman et al., 2023) or rely on estimation-based methods...

key_challenges.value: Defining process rewards is hard, online updates are not scalable, and explicit reward modeling incurs extra costs.
key_challenges.evidence: C1. Process rewards are hard to define. C2. PRM online updates are not scalable. C3. Explicit reward modeling brings extra cost.

provenance.section_header: 2.2 KEY CHALLENGES IN SCALABLE DENSE REWARDS

main_idea.value: PRIME (Process Reinforcement through Implicit Rewards) enables online PRM updates using only policy rollouts and outcome labels through implicit process rewards.
main_idea.evidence: To address these challenges, we propose PRIME (Process Reinforcement through Implicit Rewards)...

inspiration_source.value: The theoretical basis and analogies of the recording method design.
inspiration_source.evidence: This enables PRIME to perform online learning of reward signals using only outcome labels on policy rollouts...

provenance.section_header: 3.1 ENABLING SCALABLE REWARD UPDATE WITH IMPLICIT REWARD MODELING

objective.value: To train dense reward models with only outcome-level labels.
objective.evidence: At its core, the framework employs recently proposed implicit process reward modeling to train dense reward models with only outcome-level labels.

method.value: Implicit PRM enables training an ORM with outcome labels only while repurposing it as a PRM at inference.
method.evidence: We consider dense rewards from the Implicit PRM because of the scalability...

provenance.section_header: 3.1 ENABLING SCALABLE REWARD UPDATE WITH IMPLICIT REWARD MODELING

reward_calculation.value: The process rewards are obtained by r_φ(y_t) := β log(π_φ(y_t | y_<t) / π_ref(y_t | y_<t)).
reward_calculation.evidence: The process rewards are obtained by: r_φ(y_t) := β log(π_φ(y_t | y_<t) / π_ref(y_t | y_<t)).

provenance.section_header: 3.1 ENABLING SCALABLE REWARD UPDATE WITH IMPLICIT REWARD MODELING

training_objective.value: The training objective is to update the Implicit PRM online with on-policy rollouts and outcome supervision.
training_objective.evidence: In PRIME, upon rollouts being generated and graded by the (ground truth) outcome verifier, we update the Implicit PRM online...

provenance.section_header: 3.1 ENABLING SCALABLE REWARD UPDATE WITH IMPLICIT REWARD MODELING

workflow.value: The workflow involves initializing the policy model and the Implicit PRM, sampling responses, applying accuracy filters, and updating the PRM.
workflow.evidence: PRIME follows that (1) initialize policy model and the Implicit PRM both with the reference model; (2) sample multiple responses for each prompt...

key_techniques.value: PRIME combines implicit process rewards with outcome rewards into any kind of Monte Carlo advantage estimate.
key_techniques.evidence: PRIME adopts implicit process rewards for efficient PRM online update, then integrates token-level dense rewards with outcome rewards in MC advantage estimate.

provenance.section_header: 3.1 ENABLING SCALABLE REWARD UPDATE WITH IMPLICIT REWARD MODELING

inference_enhancement.value: The inference enhancement involves calculating advantages using both implicit process rewards and outcome rewards.
inference_enhancement.evidence: We calculate the return of implicit process rewards and outcome rewards separately if both are available...

experimental_setup.value: We conducted experiments with Qwen2.5-Math-7B-Base focusing on mathematical and coding problems.
experimental_setup.evidence: We focus on mathematical and coding problems in this paper. For models, we start with Qwen2.5-Math-7B-Base...

algorithm_effectiveness.value: PRIME achieves a 15.1% average improvement across several key reasoning benchmarks over the SFT model.
algorithm_effectiveness.evidence: Starting from Qwen2.5-Math-7B-Base, PRIME achieves a 15.1% average improvement across several key reasoning benchmarks...

acceleration_effect.value: PRIME achieves a 2.5x sample efficiency gain.
acceleration_effect.evidence: Compared to RL using outcome rewards only, PRIME achieves a 2.5x sample efficiency gain...

online_prm_importance.value: Updating the PRM online is key to the success of PRIME.
online_prm_importance.evidence: Our analysis shows that updating the PRM online is key to the success of PRIME.

inference_scaling_effect.value: PRIME significantly outperforms RLOO with a 6.9% performance improvement.
inference_scaling_effect.evidence: Compared with sparse reward, PRIME takes 40% of the training steps to achieve the same training rewards as RLOO and improves the final rewards by 6.9%.

main_findings.value: PRIME greatly benefits sample efficiency and policy performance and is a general method that works with broad RL algorithms.
main_findings.evidence: Throughout the experiments, we validate that PRIME (1) greatly benefits sample efficiency and policy performance, (2) is easy to use with minimum cost, and (3) is a general method that works with broad RL algorithms together.

technical_contributions.value: The innovation lies in the method extraction level through the development of PRIME.
technical_contributions.evidence: This work develops PRIME, which produces and leverages dense rewards in online RL for LLM reasoning.

practical_contributions.value: PRIME reduces the development overhead and enhances reasoning capabilities.
practical_contributions.evidence: PRIME combines well with various advantage functions and forgoes the dedicated reward model training phase that existing approaches require, substantially reducing the development overhead.

community_contributions.value: PRIME serves as a general method applicable to various RL algorithms, extending its use cases.
community_contributions.evidence: PRIME is a general plug-in for almost any RL algorithm for LLM, which largely extends the use cases of PRIME.
